# REVEL site guidance

- Preserve the existing REVEL visual language: Oswald for headings, Newsreader for body copy, warm gold accents, editorial whitespace, and a restrained ink-toned paisley treatment in header and topbar surfaces.
- Use a golden-ratio spacing scale for layout and rhythm when adjusting shared UI: `1rem`, `1.618rem`, `2.618rem`, `4.236rem`, `6.854rem`.
- Keep the header logo slightly oversized and protect surrounding whitespace instead of crowding navigation.
- For luxury South Asian wedding styling, prefer subtle ornamentation over flat fills in hero/topbar sections. Paisley should read tonal and premium, not busy or novelty-driven.
- On the team page, prefer performer logos or names only. Do not add back role-title lines such as "Jigar | Founder".
- Use portrait-friendly framing for team imagery, favoring `4 / 5` crops and careful `object-position` tweaks over forced square crops.
- Default to client-facing conversion language over planner-facing language for public marketing pages, while still preserving credibility markers.
- When rewriting bios or service copy, balance authority with outcomes: what couples get, how the experience feels, and what results are delivered.
- Prioritize modular, future-ready architecture decisions that make it easy to integrate AI features (chat support, budget calculators, venue intelligence) without a full redesign.
